target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66252: [Feature]: Per-Agent TTS/STT Configuration Overrides for Multi-Language Support
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 66252
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66252
- Title: [Feature]: Per-Agent TTS/STT Configuration Overrides for Multi-Language Support
- State: OPEN
- Author: acwilan
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

Add support for agent-specific text-to-speech (TTS) and speech-to-text (STT) configuration overrides, enabling multiple agents in a single OpenClaw instance to use different voices, languages, or providers.

### Problem to solve

Currently, TTS/STT configuration is global under messages.tts and tools.media.audio. All agents share the same voice, language, and provider settings. This prevents:

• Multi-language agents: Cannot have one agent speak English and another Spanish
• Voice differentiation: Cannot give different agents distinct voices/personalities
• Provider optimization: Cannot use different TTS providers for different use cases (e.g., fast vs. quality)

### Proposed solution

Extend the agent configuration schema to support TTS/STT overrides:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": {
      "spanish-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "Eres un asistente útil...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "macvoice",
          "voice": "Monica",  // Spanish voice
          "rate": 0.5
        },
        "stt": {
          "provider": "deepgram",
          "language": "es"
        }
      },
      "english-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "You are a helpful assistant...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "elevenlabs",
          "voice": "Adam"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Implementation notes:

• Overrides merge with global messages.tts config (agent-specific wins)
• Passed to speech providers via SpeechSynthesisRequest.providerOverrides or new agentConfig field
• Optional: Support `` directive tags as runtime override

### Alternatives considered

1. Separate OpenClaw instances: High resource overhead, complex management
2. Model-driven language detection: Unreliable, adds latency, doesn't solve voice differentiation
3. Global config switching: Requires restart, not dynamic per conversation

### Impact

• Users: Enable multi-language households, personalized agents, voice branding
• Plugins: Providers receive richer context; no breaking changes
• Ecosystem: Differentiation feature for OpenClaw vs. competitors

### Evidence/examples

• Discord communities: Spanish + English channels need different agent personalities
• Customer service: Escalation bot (professional voice) vs. casual helper (friendly voice)
• Accessibility: Different voices help users distinguish agent roles

### Additional information

• Related: macvoice plugin supports voice selection; lacks agent context
• Similar features: Agent-specific model already exists—pattern exists

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

This RFC tracks the post-#71722 finalization roadmap for the contract-first Pi/Codex runtime work.

The original RFC #71004 is implemented and closed. The core RuntimePlan boundary is now in `main`: OpenClaw-owned runtime policy is represented in `AgentRuntimePlan`, built before attempts, consumed by Pi/Codex paths, covered by parity contracts, and routed through the additive Harness V2 lifecycle.

This RFC is therefore not another GPT-5.4 bug-fix push. It is the cleanup ladder needed to make the merged architecture maintainable: native Harness V2 ownership, runner file splits by contract boundary, neutral embedded-runner naming, docs, and final smoke verification.

## Original Evidence And Merged Work

| Item | Role |
|---|---|
| #71004 | Original contract-first RFC, now closed as implemented |
| #70743 | GPT-5.4 runtime hardening point fixes |
| #70772 | Pi/Codex harness extension seams |
| #70760 | Harness selection decision observability |
| #70965 | Codex dynamic tools preserve OpenClaw hook contracts |
| #71096 | Full RuntimePlan contract suite and first implementation |
| #71722 | Consolidated RuntimePlan/Harness V2 follow-up package |
| #71196/#71197/#71201/#71220/#71222/#71223/#71224/#71238/#71239 | Superseded by #71722 and preserved there via `cherry-pick -x` |

Current high-risk structural files:

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` - about 3.1k LOC
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts` - about 2.3k LOC
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` - about 1.0k LOC
- `src/agents/harness/v2.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`
- `src/agents/runtime-plan/{types.ts,build.ts,tools.ts,auth.ts}`

## Architecture Target

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  RuntimePlan["AgentRuntimePlan\nOpenClaw-owned policy"] --> Pi["Pi embedded runner"]
  RuntimePlan --> Codex["Codex app-server adapter"]
  RuntimePlan --> Tools["Tools + schema + diagnostics"]
  RuntimePlan --> Transcript["Transcript policy"]
  RuntimePlan --> Delivery["Delivery + NO_REPLY"]
  RuntimePlan --> Outcome["Outcome + fallback"]
  RuntimePlan --> Transport["Transport params"]
  RuntimePlan --> Observability["resolvedRef events"]

  HarnessV2["Harness V2 lifecycle"] --> Pi
  HarnessV2 --> Codex
  Selection["Harness selection"] --> HarnessV2

  Pi --> SplitAttempt["attempt modules by domain"]
  Pi --> SplitRun["run orchestration modules"]
  SplitAttempt --> NeutralName["embedded-runner canonical naming"]
  SplitRun --> NeutralName
```

Desired steady state:

- OpenClaw owns runtime policy once through RuntimePlan.
- Pi and Codex consume shared policy instead of reassembling it locally.
- Harness V2 is the internal lifecycle boundary for selected harness execution.
- Public plugin `AgentHarness` remains compatible.
- `pi-embedded-runner` stays available as a compatibility path, but internal code and docs prefer neutral `embedded-runner` naming.

## Roadmap

### PR 1: Baseline Health Check

Purpose: confirm current `main` after #71722 is a clean base or document unrelated baseline drift before structural work.

Create:

```bash
git fetch origin
git switch -c contract-finalization/baseline-health origin/main
```

Change/add:

- Add `docs/refactor/runtime-plan-finalization-baseline.md`
- No production code changes unless a current-main check failure directly blocks the next PRs.

Detailed steps:

1. Run `pnpm check:architecture`.
2. Run `pnpm check:test-types`.
3. Run RuntimePlan contract tests, Harness V2 tests, Codex app-server tests, and embedded-runner focused tests.
4. Document exact failures and classify each as either current-main baseline drift or finalization-blocking.
5. If baseline drift blocks every later PR, fix that drift in this PR only and keep it separate from runtime refactors.

Suggested validation commands:

```bash
pnpm check:architecture
pnpm check:test-types
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-plan/types.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-plan/types.compat.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.diagnostics.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/v2.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
```

Open PR:

```bash
git push -u 100yenadmin contract-finalization/baseline-health
gh pr create --repo openclaw/openclaw --base main \
  --head 100yenadmin:contract-finalization/baseline-health \
  --draft \
  --title "[codex] Document RuntimePlan finalization baseline" \
  --body-file /tmp/runtime-plan-finalization-baseline-pr.md
```

### PR 2: Native Internal Harness V2 Adoption

Purpose: stop treating Harness V2 as only a generic V1 adapter while keeping public plugin `AgentHarness` compatible.

Create:

```bash
git fetch origin
git switch -c contract-finalization/native-harness-v2 origin/main
```

Change/add:

- `src/agents/harness/v2.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/builtin-pi.ts`
- `extensions/codex/harness.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/v2.test.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `extensions/codex/index.test.ts`

Detailed steps:

1. Add internal native V2 factories for built-in Pi and bundled Codex.
2. Keep `registerAgentHarness(...)` and public `AgentHarness` unchanged.
3. Update harness selection so selected harness execution uses native V2 when available, otherwise adapts V1.
4. Preserve support priority, fallback behavior, compaction, reset, dispose, cleanup, and classification semantics.
5. Add tests proving V1 harnesses still work and native V2 harnesses receive the same attempt params/result shape.

Open PR:

```bash
git push -u 100yenadmin contract-finalization/native-harness-v2
gh pr create --repo openclaw/openclaw --base main \
  --head 100yenadmin:contract-finalization/native-harness-v2 \
  --draft \
  --title "[codex] Promote Harness V2 as internal lifecycle boundary" \
  --body-file /tmp/native-harness-v2-pr.md
```

### PR 3: Split Pi Attempt Preparation Domains

Purpose: reduce `run/attempt.ts` without changing behavior.

Create:

```bash
git fetch origin
git switch -c contract-finalization/attempt-prep-split origin/main
```

Change/add:

- Add `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-tools.ts`
- Add `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-prompt.ts`
- Add `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-transport.ts`
- Keep/use `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.transcript-policy.ts`
- Update `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- Add or adjust focused tests only when extraction needs importable helpers.

Detailed steps:

1. Move tool normalization/diagnostics into `attempt-tools.ts`.
2. Move RuntimePlan prompt contribution and cache-boundary preparation into `attempt-prompt.ts`.
3. Move transport extra-param/session stream setup helpers into `attempt-transport.ts`.
4. Leave the live stream loop in `attempt.ts` for this PR.
5. RuntimePlan remains first source; legacy fallback remains.
6. No package rename, no public API rename, and no behavior changes.

Open PR:

```bash
git push -u 100yenadmin contract-finalization/attempt-prep-split
gh pr create --repo openclaw/openclaw --base main \
  --head 100yenadmin:contract-finalization/attempt-prep-split \
  --draft \
  --title "[codex] Split embedded attempt preparation domains" \
  --body-file /tmp/attempt-prep-split-pr.md
```

### PR 4: Split Pi Stream And Lifecycle Domains

Purpose: isolate the actual model loop and cleanup from transcript/session setup.

Create:

```bash
git fetch origin
git switch -c contract-finalization/attempt-stream-lifecycle-split origin/main
```

Change/add:

- Add `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-stream-loop.ts`
- Add `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-lifecycle.ts`
- Use existing `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.ts`
- Use existing `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.sessions-yield.ts`
- Use existing `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.stop-reason-recovery.ts`
- Update `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`

Detailed steps:

1. Move send/yield/tool execution loop into `attempt-stream-loop.ts`.
2. Keep recovery helpers near stream-loop state.
3. Move terminal meta, liveness, and cleanup handoff into `attempt-lifecycle.ts`.
4. Preserve all emitted events, payload/result shapes, retry behavior, and cleanup ordering.
5. Do not rename files or paths in this PR.

Open PR:

```bash
git push -u 100yenadmin contract-finalization/attempt-stream-lifecycle-split
gh pr create --repo openclaw/openclaw --base main \
  --head 100yenadmin:contract-finalization/attempt-stream-lifecycle-split \
  --draft \
  --title "[codex] Split embedded attempt stream lifecycle" \
  --body-file /tmp/attempt-stream-lifecycle-split-pr.md
```

github_context:

- Labels: P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
# Add Atomic Chat as a bundled local provider (OpenAI-compatible, 127.0.0.1:1337)

### Summary

[Atomic Chat](https://github.com/AtomicBot-ai/Atomic-Chat) is an open-source, cross-platform desktop/mobile app that runs open-weight LLMs locally and exposes a single **OpenAI-compatible** HTTP API at `http://127.0.0.1:1337/v1`. A Rust proxy in the desktop app fronts multiple inference engines (a llama.cpp/TurboQuant fork and MLX-VLM) behind that one endpoint, so clients don't need to know which backend is serving a request.

It's the same shape as the local providers OpenClaw already bundles (LM Studio, vLLM, SGLang): an OpenAI-compatible `/v1` server with `/v1/models` discovery and SSE streaming. Today users can already point OpenClaw at it via a manual custom provider, but there's no first-class entry in the provider list / onboarding.

### Proposal

Add a bundled, `enabledByDefault: true` provider plugin `atomicchat` so it shows up natively in the provider picker and onboarding, mirroring the existing `sglang` plugin:

- Provider id: `atomicchat`
- API: `openai-completions`
- Default base URL: `http://127.0.0.1:1337/v1`
- Auth env var: `ATOMIC_CHAT_API_KEY` (any non-empty value; the local server is unauthenticated on loopback by default)
- Model auto-discovery from `/v1/models`
- Docs page under `/providers/atomicchat` and an entry in the local-models guide

### Notes

- Atomic Chat already ships first-class "Launch with" presets for other agents (OpenCode, Goose, Hermes, etc.) against `localhost:1337/v1`; this issue/PR is the reverse direction (OpenClaw recognizing Atomic Chat as a provider).
- Happy to maintain the plugin.

I have a PR ready that implements this against the `sglang` template, including a local "yo" end-to-end run through the provider.

Labels: P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool
```
title: Add Atomic Chat as a bundled local provider (OpenAI-compatible, 127.0.0.1:1337)

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #37902: sessions_spawn: model overrides runTimeoutSeconds with decreasing values across retries; no config minimum enforced
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 37902
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/37902
- Title: sessions_spawn: model overrides runTimeoutSeconds with decreasing values across retries; no config minimum enforced
- State: OPEN
- Author: unw1red
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When an agent calls `sessions_spawn` to delegate to a subagent, the calling model autonomously sets `runTimeoutSeconds` in the tool call. This overrides the `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds` config value, which only acts as a **fallback default** — not a floor/minimum. The result is subagents reliably timing out even when the config specifies ample time.

## Observed Behavior

- Config: `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds: 300`
- Orchestrator model (claude-haiku-4-5) autonomously passes `runTimeoutSeconds: 60` in `sessions_spawn` calls
- On each successive retry after a timeout, the model **decreases** the value: `60 → 45 → 20 → 30 → 15`
- Each retry is given **less** time than the previous attempt, making success increasingly unlikely
- The subagent was actively making tool calls and producing output — it simply ran out of budget before finishing

## Log Evidence

```
embedded run timeout: runId=... timeoutMs=20000
Error: All models failed (2): model-a: LLM request timed out. | model-b: LLM request timed out.
lane task error: lane=subagent durationMs=60094 error="FailoverError: LLM request timed out."
```

`subagents/runs.json` entries confirm: `runTimeoutSeconds: 60`, `45`, `20`, `30`, `15` across successive spawns.

## Root Cause (from source)

```js
const cfgSubagentTimeout = cfg?.agents?.defaults?.subagents?.runTimeoutSeconds ?? 0;
const runTimeoutSeconds = typeof params.runTimeoutSeconds === "number"
  ? params.runTimeoutSeconds   // model's value wins unconditionally
  : cfgSubagentTimeout;        // config only used if model omits it
```

The model's value is accepted with no floor enforcement. The config cannot constrain what the model passes.

## Requested Feature

A config option to enforce a minimum the model cannot undercut:

- **`agents.defaults.subagents.minRunTimeoutSeconds`** — floor value the model cannot go below
- Or **`agents.defaults.subagents.lockRunTimeoutSeconds`** — config is authoritative, model value ignored

## Workaround

Instructing the orchestrating agent via system prompt to never pass `runTimeoutSeconds` in `sessions_spawn` calls works but is fragile — it relies on the model obeying the instruction every time.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
- Platform: macOS 15 (Darwin 25.3.0)
- Orchestrator model: claude-haiku-4-5
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- Sid-Qin at 2026-03-06T15:38:39Z:
Submitted a fix in PR #38048. Added `agents.defaults.subagents.minRunTimeoutSeconds` config option that enforces a minimum the model cannot undercut, preventing timeout erosion across retries.

- openclaw-barnacle at 2026-04-20T04:38:28Z:

Please add updates or it will be closed.

- clawsweeper at 2026-04-26T01:33:14Z:

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main and v2026.5.19 still implement `runTimeoutSeconds` as a fallback default only, the requested minimum/lock config surface is absent, and the linked fix PRs were closed unmerged.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: configure `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds: 120` and pass explicit `runTimeoutSeconds: 2`; the current resolver returns `2`, and the focused test asserts that behavior. I did not execute a live model retry loop.

**Next step**
The source path, config/docs contract, related attempts, and regression coverage shape are clear enough for one focused repair PR, though it is a feature/config repair rather than a strict bug-only automation candidate.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Add a backward-compatible optional `agents.defaults.subagents.minRunTimeoutSeconds` floor for native `sessions_spawn`, preserving current fallback behavior when unset and documenting zero-timeout behavior under the floor.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes, source-level reproduction is high confidence: configure `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds: 120` and pass explicit `runTimeoutSeconds: 2`; the current resolver returns `2`, and the focused test asserts that behavior. I did not execute a live model retry loop.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes, the optional minimum-floor config is the narrowest maintainable direction because it preserves released fallback semantics when unset. A lock mode or changing `runTimeoutSeconds` itself into a floor would be broader and more compatibility-sensitive.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a bounded agent/config reliability improvement with real subagent timeout impact but no emergency runtime outage.
- `impact:session-state`: The issue concerns subagent run timeout state and retry behavior for spawned sessions.

Acceptance criteria:

- pnpm config:docs:check
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/subagent-spawn-plan.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts docs/tools/subagents.md docs/gateway/config-tools.md CHANGELOG.md

([`src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts:279`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cd019cfa412b/src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts#L279), [cd019cfa412b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cd019cfa412b))
- **Docs describe default-only semantics:** The subagent docs and gateway config docs both say the config value is used when the tool call omits `runTimeoutSeconds`, not as a floor. Public docs: [`docs/tools/subagents.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/subagents). ([`docs/tools/subagents.md:150`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/subagents), [cd019cfa412b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cd019cfa412b))

- **Latest release still has fallback-only behavior:** The v2026.5.19 tag contains the same explicit-first timeout resolver and no `minRunTimeoutSeconds` or `lockRunTimeoutSeconds` matches. github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 84811
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84811
- Title: agents_list shows orphaned allowlist entries as spawnable agents; sessions_spawn accepts them without validation
- State: OPEN
- Author: lykeion-dev
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Bug Summary

When an agent configuration is deleted but its ID remains in `agents.main.subagents.allowAgents`, the `agents_list` tool continues to display it as a spawnable agent, and `sessions_spawn` successfully creates a fully functional session for it — with no warning or validation that the agent has no actual configuration.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a sub-agent with a specific ID (e.g., `my-worker`) in `agents.list[]`
2. Add `my-worker` to `agents.main.subagents.allowAgents` (the spawn allowlist)
3. Verify `agents_list` shows `my-worker` — ✅ correct
4. Delete the agent configuration from `agents.list[]`
5. **Do not** remove `my-worker` from `allowAgents` (easy to forget)
6. Call `agents_list` → `my-worker` still appears with `"configured": false`
7. Call `sessions_spawn` with `agentId: "my-worker"` → **accepted and runs successfully**

## Observed Behavior

- `agents_list` returns the orphaned ID as if it is a valid, available agent
- `sessions_spawn` creates a live session using only defaults (implicit runtime, default model)
- The spawned agent has **full host filesystem access** — no sandbox restrictions are applied
- The `"configured": false` field is ambiguous and does not clearly indicate "this agent has no configuration"

## Expected Behavior

At minimum:
- `sessions_spawn` should **reject** agent IDs that have no corresponding configuration in `agents.list[]`
- `agents_list` should either exclude orphaned entries or clearly mark them as "unconfigured — spawn will use defaults"

Ideally:
- When an agent config is removed from `agents.list[]`, its entry should be auto-removed from `allowAgents`
- Or: `agents_list` should distinguish between "configured agent" and "allowlist-only entry"

## Security Impact

An agent that was intentionally removed (e.g., due to permission issues, decommissioning, or misconfiguration) can be respawned by any session with spawn access, inheriting default permissions with no explicit sandbox or tool restrictions. This is a **privilege boundary bypass** — the operator believes the agent is gone, but it persists as a ghost in the allowlist.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.5.12
- Node.js v22.22.0
- Ubuntu 22.04

## Workaround

Manually audit `agents.main.subagents.allowAgents` and remove any IDs that no longer have a corresponding entry in `agents.list[]`.
```

This PR adds a provider-agnostic voice-persistence mechanism to the TTS subsystem. The LLM can include `persist=true` in a `[[tts:...]]` directive; after a successful TTS call the chosen voice is written to the user prefs file (keyed by provider) and automatically applied to every subsequent call until changed or cleared. The implementation is clean and follows existing patterns (`getTtsProvider`/`setTtsProvider`), and the 12 new tests provide solid coverage. **Issues found:** - **Telephony path silently ignores persisted voice** (`tts.ts` line ~820): `textToSpeechTelephony` passes `config.elevenlabs.voiceId` / `config.openai.voice` directly to the TTS calls without consulting `getTtsVoice`. The standard `textToSpeech` path was correctly updated; the telephony path was missed. - **`persist` token not gated by `allowVoice` policy** (`tts-core.ts` line 327): Every other voice-related directive key checks `if (!policy.allowVoice) { break; }` before taking action. The `persist` case sets the flag unconditionally. This is harmless today (no voice override enters `overrides` when `allowVoice` is false), but breaks the policy-checking invariant. - **`getTtsVoice` uses `||` instead of `??`** (`tts.ts` line 505): Could mishandle a manually-authored `""` entry in the JSON prefs file that `setTtsVoice` would otherwise prevent. - **`getTtsVoice`/`setTtsVoice` unnecessarily duplicated in `_test`** (`tts.ts` lines 1036-1037): Both are already public named exports; only private helpers like `resolveVoiceOverrideForPersist` need the `_test` escape hatch.

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a lifecycle hook system for cron jobs, allowing user-defined scripts to run at `beforeRun`, `afterComplete`, `onFailure`, and `afterRun` points. The implementation is largely solid — filter logic, priority sorting, timeout cleanup, and error isolation are well-designed and match the previous review's fixes. However, there is one logic inconsistency and a few smaller concerns worth addressing before merge. - **`afterRun` hooks do not fire when `beforeRun` aborts** — the early return in `timer.ts` exits before reaching the `afterRun` block, contradicting the stated guarantee that `afterRun` always fires. Hook authors relying on `afterRun` for cleanup or audit logging will silently miss it when a `beforeRun` hook aborts. - **`CronHookContext.result` field is defined but never populated** — hook scripts that access `ctx.result` will always receive `undefined`; the field should be removed or actually set in the context builder. - **`runCronHooks` abort path has no unit test** — the core `beforeRun` abort behaviour (returning `{ abort: true }`) is not directly exercised in the test suite despite being the most security-sensitive path in the feature.

The dead `result` field and missing abort test reduce confidence in the completeness of the change. - `src/cron/service/timer.ts` — the `beforeRun` abort early-return path needs to be restructured so `afterRun` hooks still fire.

If an `afterRun` hook is used for cleanup, audit logging, or metrics recording, it will be silently missed whenever a `beforeRun` hook aborts the job. GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 63057 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63057 - Title: fix(anthropic): replace `|| 1024` fallback with safe `budget_tokens` guard - State: OPEN - Author: Zeesejo - Labels: agents, size: XS, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 availability, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Problem — issue #63016 In `buildAnthropicParams` (`src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`), the legacy budget-based thinking block was built with: ```ts params.thinking = { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: options.thinkingBudgetTokens || 1024, }; ``` The `||` operator coerces **any falsy value** — including the legitimate `0` produced by `adjustMaxTokensForThinking` when `maxTokens` is too small — to `1024`. This silently activates deprecated `type: "enabled"` thinking with a non-zero budget on every API call, even trivial ones (file reads, short messages), wasting tokens and bypassing user intent. A secondary problem: when `adjustMaxTokensForThinking` returns `thinkingBudget: 0`, sending `budget_tokens: 0` to the Anthropic API results in a HTTP 400 error. ## Fix Two-line change in `buildAnthropicParams`: ```ts // Before params.thinking = { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: options.thinkingBudgetTokens || 1024, }; // After const budgetTokens = options.thinkingBudgetTokens ?? 1024; if (budgetTokens > 0) { params.thinking = { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: budgetTokens, }; } ``` 1. **`?? 1024`** — nullish coalescing preserves an explicit `0` instead of coercing it to `1024`. 2. **`if (budgetTokens > 0)`** — when the token envelope is too small for thinking (budget = 0), the `thinking` block is omitted entirely rather than sending an API-rejecting `budget_tokens: 0`. All well-formed thinking configurations (explicit positive budgets, resolved non-zero budgets) are unaffected. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-08T09:00:36Z:

Fixes a `|| 1024` falsy-coercion bug in `buildAnthropicParams` where an explicit `thinkingBudgetTokens: 0` (produced by `adjustMaxTokensForThinking` when the token envelope is too small) was being silently promoted to `1024`, and adds a `budgetTokens > 0` guard to avoid sending `budget_tokens: 0` to the Anthropic API, which returns an HTTP 400. The change is correct and well-commented; no tests were added to cover the fixed `budget_tokens === 0` path or the `undefined → 1024` fallback.

The logic change itself is correct, well-reasoned, and properly commented. No P0/P1 issues. src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts — no test coverage was added for the fixed code path.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified.

That makes account-only config edits broader and more disruptive than necessary. ## Tests Added coverage for: - account-scoped channel account config -> account-only restart - channel-global config -> full channel restart - mixed account-scoped + channel-global changes -> full channel restart wins - multiple account-scoped changes in one channel -> collect all impacted accounts Validated with: ```bash corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts ``` ## Scope / non-goals This change is intentionally narrow. It does not redesign active reply draining, typing lifecycle coordination, or provider-specific recovery behavior. Closes #43935 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-12T10:31:01Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR narrows gateway hot-reload scope by introducing account-scoped channel restarts. Previously, any config change under `channels..*` triggered a full channel restart; the change teaches the reload planner to detect paths matching `channels..accounts..*` and instead restart only the affected account via the existing `stopChannel(name, accountId)` / `startChannel(name, accountId)` lifecycle hooks. Full-channel restarts are preserved for channel-global config changes, and a post-processing cleanup step ensures a mixed batch (account-scoped + channel-global for the same channel) always collapses back to a full restart — so the new path is strictly narrower, never broader. Key changes: - `GatewayReloadPlan` gains a `restartChannelAccounts: Map>` field; `buildGatewayReloadPlan` populates it from scoped paths and clears conflicting entries when a full restart is also planned for the same channel. - `createGatewayReloadHandlers` wires up account-scoped restarts before the existing full-channel loop; the `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS` / `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS` guards continue to apply to both paths. - Four new unit tests in `config-reload.test.ts` cover each decision branch, and an integration test in `server.reload.test.ts` confirms `stopChannel`/`startChannel` are called with the correct `accountId`. - All pre-existing plan fixtures in `server.reload.test.ts` are updated with `restartChannelAccounts: new Map()` to stay in sync with the type change.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge; changes are narrowly scoped, logically correct, and well covered by tests. - The plan-building logic correctly handles all four cases (account-scoped only, channel-global only, mixed, multi-account) via clear branching and a post-loop deduplication step. The handler wiring is additive and consistent with existing patterns. No regressions are introduced to full-channel restart behavior, and test coverage directly exercises the new code paths end-to-end. - No files require special attention. The logic is sound: account-only config changes get account restart; channel-global changes get full channel restart; mixed changes default to full restart. Good prioritization. Test coverage looks comprehensive. Ready to merge pending CI. - coppynight at 2026-03-13T23:28:39Z: This is a narrow gateway reload fix. It only changes hot-reload planning for `channels..accounts..*`, while preserving full-channel restart precedence for channel-global or mixed changes. Added regression coverage in: - `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` - `src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` CI is green. Happy to adjust if you'd prefer a different reload-plan shape or want this scoped differently. - openclaw-barnacle at 2026-04-27T04:42:26Z: This pull request has been automatically marked as stale due to inactivity. github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 47859 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/47859 - Title: feat(tui): add image attachment support via @path syntax and /image command - State: OPEN - Author: skillhq-ai - Labels: size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Add `@path` syntax for inline image attachments in TUI chat messages (e.g. `describe this @./screenshot.png`) - Add `/image` slash command for attaching images to the next message - Extract image path parsing into dedicated `tui-image-extract.ts` module with full test coverage ## Test plan - [ ] Verify `@./path/to/image.png` syntax extracts image and sends with message - [ ] Verify `/image path/to/image.png` command attaches image to next message - [ ] Run `pnpm test -- src/tui/tui-image-extract.test.ts` to confirm unit tests pass - [ ] Test with various image formats (png, jpg, gif, webp) - [ ] Test error handling for non-existent files 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-16T03:54:42Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds image attachment support to the TUI via two mechanisms: an `@path` inline syntax for embedding images in messages, and a `/image ` slash command. A new `tui-image-extract.ts` module handles path extraction and file loading, with solid unit-test coverage for both happy paths and error cases. **Key findings:** - **Logic bug** (`tui-command-handlers.ts:565`): When the entire message is an image reference (e.g. `@./screenshot.png` with no other text), `extractImagePaths` returns `cleanText: ""`. The `cleanText || text` fallback then sends the raw `@`-path string to the gateway and shows it in the chat log instead of a useful placeholder. - **TOCTOU race** (`tui-image-extract.ts:88–101`): File size is checked with `fs.stat` then the file is read with a separate `fs.readFile` call. Reading the buffer first and checking `buffer.byteLength` eliminates the race and reduces syscalls. - **Incorrect ESLint disable** (`tui-image-extract.ts:29`): The `// eslint-disable-next-line no-control-regex` comment does not apply to the regex below it (no control characters are used); it should be removed.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Not safe to merge as-is — one logic bug causes the raw `@path` syntax to be sent to the gateway when no other text accompanies the image reference. - The feature is well-structured and has good test coverage, but the `cleanText || text` fallback in `sendMessage` is a real logic bug that will produce confusing UX and wrong message content for a common use case. The other two issues (TOCTOU and lint comment) are minor. Fixing the message fallback is needed before merging. - `src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts` (line 565) requires the `cleanText || text` fallback fix; `src/tui/tui-image-extract.ts` has a minor TOCTOU and an incorrect ESLint suppression.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts Line: 565 Comment: **`@path`-only message sends raw path syntax to gateway** When the entire message is an image reference (e.g. the user types `@./screenshot.png` with no accompanying text), `extractImagePaths` returns `cleanText: ""`. The `||` fallback then sets `message = text`, which is the original raw string `@./screenshot.png`. This raw `@`-reference is then shown in the chat log via `chatLog.addUser(message)` and sent to the gateway, instead of an empty string or a meaningful placeholder. ```suggestion message = cleanText !== "" ? cleanText : "[image attached]"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/tui/tui-image-extract.ts Line: 88-101 Comment: **TOCTOU race between `stat` and `readFile`** The file's size is checked via `fs.stat`, but the file is read separately with `fs.readFile`. In the window between the two calls the file could be replaced or appended to, so a file that passes the 5 MB guard could ultimately be read at a larger size. Reading the buffer first and then checking `buffer.byteLength` eliminates the race and avoids an extra syscall: ```typescript let buffer: Buffer; try { buffer = await fs.readFile(resolved); } catch { throw new Error(`file not found: ${rawPath}`); } if (buffer.byteLength > MAX_IMAGE_BYTES) { throw new Error( `file too large: ${rawPath} (${buffer.byteLength} bytes, max ${MAX_IMAGE_BYTES} bytes)`, ); } const content = buffer.toString("base64"); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/tui/tui-image-extract.ts Line: 29 Comment: **Incorrect ESLint disable comment** `no-control-regex` flags regex patterns that match Unicode control characters (code points U+0000–U+001F). The `IMAGE_REF_RE` regex contains no such patterns — it only uses ordinary escape sequences like `\\.` and `\\s`. The suppression comment is unnecessary and misleading; it should be removed to avoid masking a real lint warning in the future. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
- Extract the macOS CLI root-command parser/dispatcher into a small testable helper.
- Keep the existing `openclaw-mac` runtime behavior while switching the entrypoint to the resolved action enum.
- Add Swift Testing coverage for empty args, help aliases, subcommand argument splitting, known command dispatch, and case-sensitive unknown commands.

Fixes #83879

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `parseRootCommand` and the `OpenClawMacCLI.main()` root dispatch table had no unit-testable surface, so regressions in empty args, help aliases, argument splitting, known command dispatch, or unknown-command handling would not be caught.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Apple Swift 6.1 installed locally, branch `test/macos-cli-root-command-dispatch-83879`. The package manifest requires Swift tools 6.2, so full `swift test` is expected to run in macOS CI rather than this local Swift 6.1 toolchain.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ swift /tmp/openclaw-root-parser-proof.swift
$ cd apps/macos
$ swiftc -parse Sources/OpenClawMacCLI/EntryPoint.swift Sources/OpenClawMacCLI/RootCommandParser.swift
$ swiftc -parse Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/RootCommandParserTests.swift
$ swift test --filter RootCommandParserTests
$ cd ../..
$ git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: The standalone Swift proof using the same root-command parser/dispatcher logic passed:

```console
root command parser proof passed
```

The changed source and test files also passed Swift parser checks with `swiftc -parse`, and `git diff --check` passed. The local full SwiftPM test command reported the expected toolchain mismatch:

```console
error: 'macos': package 'macos' is using Swift tools version 6.2.0 but the installed version is 6.1.0
```

Observed result after fix: The proof confirms empty args resolve to usage, help aliases resolve to usage, subcommand arguments are preserved after the root command, `configure-remote` dispatches with its arguments, and `Connect` remains a case-sensitive unknown command.

What was not tested: I did not run the full macOS SwiftPM test target locally because the installed Swift toolchain is 6.1 while `apps/macos/Package.swift` requires Swift tools 6.2. CI's macOS Swift lane should provide the required toolchain and run `RootCommandParserTests` as part of the package tests.

## Test plan
- `swift /tmp/openclaw-root-parser-proof.swift`
- `swiftc -parse Sources/OpenClawMacCLI/EntryPoint.swift Sources/OpenClawMacCLI/RootCommandParser.swift` from `apps/macos`
- `swiftc -parse Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/RootCommandParserTests.swift` from `apps/macos`
- `git diff --check`

```

The recently-added `src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test-helpers.ts` (commit 905d2d8062, `test: share qa runtime fixtures`) returns an object built from `vi.fn()` calls whose inferred type references `@vitest/spy`'s `Procedure`, which is not in a path TS can portably emit in declarations. Test helpers shaped as `*.test-helpers.ts` are vitest fixtures and have no business being part of the public plugin-sdk type surface. ## Fix Add `"src/**/*.test-helpers.ts"` to the exclude list in `tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json`. target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #78303: feat(mcp): channel-mediated approval for MCP tool calls (consent envelope)
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_pr
- Number: 78303
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/78303
- Title: feat(mcp): channel-mediated approval for MCP tool calls (consent envelope)
- State: OPEN
- Author: oalterg
- Labels: docs, gateway, agents, size: XL, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, mantis: telegram-visible-proof, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look

Body:
```markdown
Closes #78308

## Summary

Today, when a bundle-MCP tool returns to the agent, OpenClaw passes the result straight through to the model — there's no approval gate analogous to the one [`exec-approvals`](../tools/exec-approvals.md) provides for shell commands. For act-tier MCP tools (send email, create vault entry, set HA state, etc.), the only thing standing between the user and the action is *the model deciding to call it*.

This PR adds a small, additive contract — the **MCP consent envelope** — that lets MCP servers opt into the existing `/approve <id> allow-once|allow-always|deny` pipeline that already backs shell-exec approvals. **Servers that don't opt in are unaffected.**

## The contract

When a server wants user approval for a call, it returns:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "requires_confirmation": true,
  "action_id": "<server-side single-use token>",
  "summary": "<one-line description of the action>",
  "expires_in_seconds": 60
}
```

…in either `structuredContent` or as JSON inside `content[0].text`. OpenClaw recognises the envelope, suppresses the result, and:

1. Issues a `plugin.approval.request` to the local gateway (reuses everything: `channel-approval-auth`, ID-prefix routing, the magic-word reply parser).
2. Blocks until the user replies on the trusted channel.
3. On `allow-once` / `allow-always`: re-calls the tool with `confirmation_token = action_id` set on the input. Returns that second result to the agent.
4. On `deny` / expiry / error: returns a synthetic `{ok:false, approved:false, reason}` result. The model never sees `action_id`.

## Why the trust boundary actually moves

The model never sees `action_id` and **`confirmation_token` is stripped from any model-supplied input** on the first call. Even if a malicious or careless agent fabricates a token, the MCP server's redemption check rejects it (the server is responsible for one-shot/TTL enforcement of `action_id`).

This is the same pattern OpenClaw already enforces for shell exec via `exec-approvals` — *agent asks, user authorises, runtime executes*. The PR just generalises that pattern to MCP tools that opt into the contract.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-consent.ts` | **New.** Envelope detection, the default approval requester (calls `plugin.approval.request` + `waitDecision`), `confirmation_token` scrubber, denied-result builder, **`sanitiseToolEmittedApprovalText`** (review-comment defence — neutralises `/approve` in MCP-emitted text). ~270 lines. |
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts` | Wraps every tool's `execute()` in a new `callMcpToolWithConsent` orchestrator. Plumbs `requestApproval`, `consentEnabled`, **`agentId`, and `sessionKey`** through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun` and `createBundleMcpToolRuntime`. |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`, `compact.ts` | Pass the agent harness's existing `agentId` + `sessionKey` into the materialize layer so the gateway forwarder can resolve the user's actual delivery channel (WhatsApp / Telegram / Slack / web UI). |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.ts` | Accept bare `/approve allow-once` (no id) when there's exactly one pending approval — typing a uuid on a phone is unrealistic UX. On ambiguity (>1 pending), refuses with a hint including the explicit form. |
| `src/infra/plugin-approvals.ts` | Cleaner reply prompt: shows all three decisions (`allow-once \| allow-always \| deny`) inline, copy-paste-ready, with the id on its own line for the explicit fallback. |
| `src/config/types.mcp.ts` | Adds `mcp.approvals.enabled?: boolean` (default true). |
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-consent.test.ts` | **New.** 21 unit tests including a live-caught `decision: null` regression and a `agentId/sessionKey` propagation regression. |
| `docs/tools/mcp-consent-envelope.md` | **New.** Server-author contract, configuration, trust-boundary explanation. |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Entry under `Unreleased`. |

No new approval kind is introduced — the implementation **reuses the existing `"plugin"` ApprovalKind**.

## Test plan

- ✅ `pnpm install`
- ✅ `pnpm lint` — 8234 files, 213 rules, 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- ✅ Full agents-core suite: **272 files passed (272), 3515 tests passed | 4 skipped**.
- ✅ `pi-bundle-mcp-consent.test.ts` — 21/21 green.
- ✅ `commands-approve.test.ts` — 18/18 green (existing tests still pass after the implicit-id parser change).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Without channel-mediated approval, the model is the trust gate for MCP tool calls that want user approval. Captured live on **vanilla `OpenClaw 2026.4.24`** with HomeBrain's MCP servers: when the agent fired `homebrain-nextcloud__nc-files_share`, the consent envelope (with `action_id`) passed straight to the model, which **leaked the token verbatim into chat**, **offered to self-confirm**, and **hallucinated a second action_id off-by-one** when it lost track of the first. Full screenshot + analysis: [#78308 comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4391533882). This PR replaces that prompt-level honour-system with runtime-enforced channel approval.

**Environment tested:** `homebrain@192.168.178.58`. x86_64 Ubuntu 24.04. AMD Radeon RX 9060 XT (16 GB VRAM). llama.cpp + Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (Q5_K_XL). HomeBrain reference MCP servers from [oalterg/HomeBrain](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain). **OpenClaw built directly from this PR branch and installed live** in place of the npm-shipped 2026.4.24 binary for the runtime-side tests.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
// Local: build + pack the PR branch
pnpm build && npm pack
// Deploy the .tgz to the target box
scp openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz homebrain@192.168.178.58:/tmp/
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'echo admin | sudo -S bash -c "
  cd /usr/lib/node_modules
  rm -rf openclaw && mkdir openclaw && cd openclaw
  tar xzf /tmp/openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz --strip-components=1
  npm install --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund --silent"'
// Set the missing approvals.plugin config (the forwarder needs it to deliver)
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'sudo -u homebrain jq ".approvals = {plugin:{enabled:true,mode:\"session\"}}" \
  ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c && sudo -u homebrain cp /tmp/c ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'sudo -u homebrain systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway'

// User sends on WhatsApp:
//   "Create a public share link to /uploads in Nextcloud, expires in 7 days."
// OpenClaw routes through the agent harness, which fires nc.files_share.
// My code intercepts the consent envelope, calls plugin.approval.request.
// The forwarder delivers the prompt to the user's WhatsApp + web UI.
// User taps Allow once on the web UI (or replies /approve allow-once on WhatsApp).
// Runtime re-calls the tool with confirmation_token. Real share URL returns.
```

**Evidence after fix:**

The web UI's native plugin-approval modal renders my PR's `plugin.approval.request` exactly right — title `MCP tool approval`, description `homebrain-nextcloud.nc.files_share — Nextcloud: create public share link for /uploads (expires in 7 days)`, severity `warning`, plugin `mcp:homebrain-nextcloud`, three buttons `Allow once / Always allow / Deny`, expiry countdown. The user pressed `Allow once`. Screenshot captured (not committed; happy to attach for maintainer review). Same approval was offered on the user's WhatsApp simultaneously via the routing-fix commit.

```text
$ openclaw --version  # the deployed PR build
OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (a2cd999)

// Gateway log (verbatim) at the moment the user tapped Allow once:
2026-05-06T20:01:22 ⇄ res ✓ plugin.approval.waitDecision 59412ms
                    conn=6f5914ca…dfe2 id=0c6f887a…7e6d

// /var/log/homebrain/mcp-nextcloud-audit.log — the result of the consent loop:
2026-05-06T20:01:22 {"server":"nextcloud","action":"share",
                     "path":"/uploads","expire_days":7}

// Nextcloud confirms the actual share resource exists on disk:
$ curl -u admin:<app-pass> -H 'OCS-APIRequest: true' \
  http://127.0.0.1:8080/ocs/v2.php/apps/files_sharing/api/v1/shares
{"id":"2","share_type":3,"path":"/uploads","stime":1778097682,
 "url":"http://127.0.0.1:8080/s/iiaRr54CKLnZ7sC", ...}
```

**Observed result after the fix:**

- **Vanilla baseline (without PR):** the model leaks `action_id` to chat, offers to self-confirm, hallucinates a second action_id off-by-one. Trust boundary is the model's behavior — soft gate. [Screenshot + analysis in #78308 comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4391533882).
- **PR build:** the agent fires the tool, the consent envelope is intercepted, `plugin.approval.request` is issued to the gateway with the correct `agentId` + `sessionKey` so the forwarder can route. Approval prompt arrives in the **web UI's native modal** (gorgeous render, all three decisions one tap) AND on the user's **WhatsApp** simultaneously. User taps `Allow once` (or replies `/approve allow-once`). Runtime re-calls the tool wi

[truncated]
```

## Summary

- allow `google-vertex` as a configured model provider/model `api` value
- regenerate the base config schema so `openclaw config validate` accepts native Vertex provider configs
- add coverage that native Vertex configs validate and keep their Vertex transport during Google provider normalization

OpenClaw already registers the bundled Google Vertex provider and can resolve ADC auth evidence with the `gcp-vertex-credentials` marker, but the config schema did not allow `api: "google-vertex"`. Users configuring Vertex manually were pushed toward the OpenAI-compatible endpoint with `api: "openai-completions"`, where the ADC marker can be treated like a literal key/header value instead of the native Vertex ADC flow.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #49120: fix(security): reject private/internal hosts in cron webhook URLs
github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 49120 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/49120 - Title: fix(security): reject private/internal hosts in cron webhook URLs - State: OPEN - Author: frankentini - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Problem `normalizeHttpWebhookUrl` only validated that webhook URLs used http(s) protocol, but did not check whether the hostname pointed to private, loopback, link-local, or other special-use addresses. A cron job configured with `delivery.mode="webhook"` could target: - `localhost` / `127.0.0.1` / `[::1]` - RFC 1918 private ranges (`10.x`, `172.16.x`, `192.168.x`) - Cloud metadata endpoints (`169.254.169.254`, `metadata.google.internal`) - `.local` / `.internal` hostnames While the runtime fetch layer performs DNS-pinned SSRF validation, rejecting these at URL creation time prevents dangerous URLs from being persisted in cron job configs in the first place. ## Fix Import and apply the existing `isBlockedHostnameOrIp` guard from the SSRF module (`src/infra/net/ssrf.ts`) inside `normalizeHttpWebhookUrl`. target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #58887: feat(typing): show typing indicator immediately on voice message receipt, before STT transcription
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 58887
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/58887
- Title: feat(typing): show typing indicator immediately on voice message receipt, before STT transcription
- State: OPEN
- Author: richardhxwang
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

When a user sends a voice message, the typing indicator only appears **after** STT transcription completes — not when the message is received. This creates a silent wait of 3–6 seconds before any feedback is shown:

| Step | Time |
|------|------|
| Telegram polling + message received | ~1s |
| Audio file download from Telegram CDN | ~2–3s |
| STT transcription (e.g. OpenAI gpt-4o-mini-transcribe) | ~1–2s |
| **Typing indicator finally appears** | **~4–6s after send** |

For text messages, typing shows in ~1s. The voice UX feels broken by comparison.

## Expected Behavior

Typing indicator should fire **as soon as the voice message is received**, before the transcription pipeline starts — similar to how `typingMode: "instant"` works for text messages.

## Suggested Implementation

In the inbound voice message handler, send `sendChatAction(typing)` immediately upon receiving the message, before dispatching to the STT pipeline. This is purely a UX change with no functional impact.

## Context

- Channel: Telegram (likely affects WhatsApp, WeChat, and other channels with voice support)
- `typingMode: "instant"` is already configured but has no effect on voice messages because the agent loop doesn't start until after transcription
- Related: #39052 (parallelize audio preflight), #39075 (optimize Telegram pipeline)
```

Additionally, on v2026.4.26 (the reported version), the older wrapper code and codex-acp 0.11.x dependency had different arg handling that contributed to the failure. Current main already improved the wrapper normalization and upgraded to codex-acp 0.12.0, but the duplicate-append issue remained. ## Changes - **`extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`**: Add `commandHasConfigKey()` that checks if a command string already contains a `-c =...` arg. Modified `appendCodexAcpConfigOverrides()` to skip appending a `-c` override if that config key is already present in the command string, preventing duplication. - **`extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`**: Added tests covering: - Configured command with both `-c model` and `-c model_reasoning_effort` is not duplicated - Partial overlap: only missing keys are appended - Wrapper command variant with pre-existing `-c` args - `commandHasConfigKey` utility behavior - The #74305 scenario: configured command with `-c` overrides, no runtime model passed Fixes #74305 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-29T12:56:52Z:

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 64718 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64718 - Title: fix(security): default exec to deny for non-owner auto-reply senders - State: OPEN - Author: sumleo - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Problem: Exec tool defaults (`security="full"`, `ask="off"`) applied identically regardless of whether the trigger was an operator CLI session or an anonymous channel auto-reply. In auto-reply mode there is no UI to approve exec commands, so `ask="off"` means no approval gate exists for unauthenticated senders. - Why it matters: Unauthenticated channel message senders could trigger arbitrary command execution via prompt injection. - What changed: `resolveReplyExecOverrides` now accepts a `senderIsOwner` parameter. When no explicit exec overrides are configured and the sender is not an owner (`senderIsOwner === false`), the function returns `{ security: "deny", ask: "always" }` instead of `undefined` (which would fall through to the permissive global defaults). - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Owner sessions and explicitly configured agent/session/directive overrides are unaffected. Backward compatibility is preserved when `senderIsOwner` is not provided (legacy callers). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Security hardening ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Skills / tool execution - [x] Auth / tokens ## Linked Issue/PR - Related OC-2026-004 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: `resolveReplyExecOverrides` returned `undefined` when no overrides were set, causing the caller to fall through to global defaults (`security="full"`, `ask="off"`) for all senders equally, including unauthenticated channel users. - Missing detection / guardrail: No sender-awareness in the exec override resolution path. - Contributing context (if known): The exec defaults were designed for CLI/owner use; the auto-reply path reused them without distinguishing sender trust level. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-exec-overrides.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: Non-owner sender with no explicit overrides gets `{ security: "deny", ask: "always" }`. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The unit test directly exercises the resolution function with each sender ownership state. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None previously. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Non-owner channel senders now default to `security="deny"` and `ask="always"` for exec tools when no explicit exec overrides are configured. This effectively blocks command execution for unauthenticated auto-reply senders by default. - Operators can still explicitly configure exec overrides (via agent defaults, session directives, or inline directives) to grant exec access to non-owner senders if desired. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: [non-owner channel msg] -> resolveReplyExecOverrides -> undefined -> global defaults (security=full, ask=off) -> exec allowed After: [non-owner channel msg] -> resolveReplyExecOverrides -> { security: "deny", ask: "always" } -> exec blocked ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes - Data access scope changed? No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The change restricts the exec surface for non-owner senders. Risk is minimal since it only tightens defaults; operators retain explicit override capability. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Any channel auto-reply surface ### Steps 1. Configure an agent with no explicit exec overrides 2. Send a message from a non-owner channel sender that triggers exec tool use 3. Observe exec is now denied by default ### Expected - Non-owner senders get exec denied ### Actual - Previously: exec was allowed with full security and no ask gate ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after 7 tests pass including 4 new tests covering all `senderIsOwner` states. ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: All 7 unit tests pass, covering owner/non-owner/undefined/explicit-override cases - Edge cases checked: `senderIsOwner` undefined (legacy backward compat), non-owner with explicit agent defaults (operator config takes precedence) - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end auto-reply flow with a live channel ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Operators who intentionally allow non-owner exec via implicit defaults will see exec denied after this change. - Mitigation: They can set explicit agent exec defaults (`tools.exec.security` / `tools.exec.ask`) to restore the previous behavior. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-11T08:42:54Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds `senderIsOwner` awareness to `resolveReplyExecOverrides` so that non-owner channel senders default to `security=\"deny\"` / `ask=\"always\"` for exec tools, hardening against prompt-injection–driven command execution. The one-line call-site change in `get-reply-directives.ts` correctly forwards `command.senderIsOwner` (always a `boolean`). - **Incomplete deny guard**: the deny path only fires when every override field is falsy, but `security` is sourced from `directives.execSecurity` first. A non-owner who includes `/exec security=full` in their message populates that field and bypasses the guard entirely. `persistInlineDirectives` has no authorization check, so the injected setting also persists to the session store for future turns. - The tests cover the \"no overrides\" scenarios well but lack a case for inline exec directive injection by a non-owner sender, which would expose the bypass.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The PR is a meaningful security improvement but contains a P1 bypass that should be addressed before merging. The deny default closes the primary prompt-injection path, but the senderIsOwner guard is evaluated only after directives.execSecurity is already resolved, leaving a direct bypass via inline /exec security=full injection. Because persistInlineDirectives also saves exec fields without an auth check, the bypass survives across session turns. The fix is straightforward but the current implementation does not fully achieve its stated security goal. src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-exec-overrides.ts — the senderIsOwner check must be hoisted before directive/session override resolution, or the logic should be restructured to only honor agentExecDefaults for non-owner senders.

This partially undermines the security goal of the PR.

A non-owner sender who includes `/exec security=full` in their message body populates `directives.execSecurity`, making the condition false and returning `{ security: "full" }` instead of the intended deny. Because `persistInlineDirectives` also saves exec fields to the session store for non-internal channels (no sender-auth guard), the bypass persists across future turns too. github_context:

- Title: cron announce delivery triggers EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError when user is actively chatting
- State: OPEN
- Author: jonah791
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

When a cron job (sessionTarget="isolated") finishes and its announce delivery sends the result to the same Telegram user, it triggers:

EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released

## Root cause

1. User sends a Telegram message -> session file locked for processing
2. LLM call is made -> embedded prompt lock temporarily released
3. Cron job completes -> announce delivery writes to the same Telegram session (delivery.to = same user ID)
4. Session file changes while the original LLM call is still in flight
5. LLM returns -> tries to resume -> detects file changed -> takeover error

## Relevant config

Cron job delivery:
{
  "sessionTarget": "isolated",
  "delivery": {
    "mode": "announce",
    "channel": "telegram",
    "to": "8790460537"
  }
}

## Expected behavior

Cron announce delivery should not modify the active user session file, or should use a separate write path that doesn't conflict with the embedded agent's prompt lock.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.5.18
- Telegram channel
- Isolated cron jobs with announce delivery

```

**Summary**
Keep open: current main still has a plausible source path where explicit isolated cron announce delivery can append a delivery-mirror message into a resolved per-channel-peer Telegram session, while the embedded runner treats any external transcript change during the released prompt lock as a takeover.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: an isolated cron job with explicit Telegram announce delivery can resolve to a per-channel-peer user session and append a delivery-mirror transcript, while the embedded runner explicitly throws if that session file changes during releaseForPrompt. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.

**Next step**
This is a narrow, source-traceable session concurrency bug in cron delivery mirroring with clear likely files and focused regression coverage.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep the direct channel send, but defer or queue the cron completion awareness for the resolved active destination session instead of immediately appending a delivery-mirror transcript when an isolated cron job targets a chat session that may be in flight.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes, by source inspection: an isolated cron job with explicit Telegram announce delivery can resolve to a per-channel-peer user session and append a delivery-mirror transcript, while the embedded runner explicitly throws if that session file changes during releaseForPrompt. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes, the best fix is likely in the cron delivery mirror/awareness path, not in Telegram sending or by weakening the embedded session takeover guard. The fix should preserve the visible channel send while avoiding immediate transcript mutation of a possibly active destination session.

github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 17925
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/17925
- Title: [Feature]: Support native web_search passthrough for ZAI (GLM) and Google (Gemini) providers
- State: OPEN
- Author: cedar202510-dotcom
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# [Feature]: Support native web_search passthrough for ZAI (GLM) and Google (Gemini) providers

### Summary

### Feature Description

OpenClaw already supports native `web_search` tool passthrough for xAI Grok via the Responses API (see `runGrokSearch` in the source code, which sends `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`). 

**Request:** Please add the same native web search passthrough support for **ZAI (Zhipu GLM)** and **Google Gemini** providers, both of which offer free, built-in web search tools in their APIs.

### Why This Matters

Currently, the only way to enable web search in OpenClaw is through the `tools.web.search` configuration, which requires a third-party search API key (Brave, Perplexity, or Grok). However:

- **ZAI/GLM** natively supports `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` in the API request — it's free and included with the model API
- **Google Gemini** natively supports `tools: [{ google_search: {} }]` — also free

Both of these providers are already built-in to OpenClaw, so users already have valid API keys configured. There's no reason to require a separate paid search API when the model provider offers search for free.

### Current Behavior

When using GLM-5 (zai provider), the agent tells users "I cannot search the web" because OpenClaw doesn't include the native `web_search` tool in the API request body.

### Expected Behavior

When the model provider is `zai` and the user hasn't configured a separate web search provider, OpenClaw should automatically include `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` in the API request to leverage GLM's native search capability. Similarly for Gemini with `google_search`.

### Reference

- ZAI `web_search` API docs: https://open.bigmodel.cn/dev/api/search/web-search-pro
- Gemini Google Search grounding: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding
- Existing Grok implementation: `runGrokSearch()` in `reply-*.js` already does `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`
- Related PRs: #10454 (Zhipu web search plugin, closed), #13814 (ZAI Search provider, open)

### Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.2.15
- Provider: zai (GLM-5), google (Gemini 2.5 Flash/Pro)

### Problem to solve

OpenClaw 在调用智谱 GLM 和 Google Gemini 模型时，没有传递这些模型 API 原生自带的免费搜索工具，导致 agent 无法联网搜索。

具体表现：使用 GLM-5 作为默认模型时，agent 回复"我没有办法进行搜索"。但智谱 API 原生支持在请求中加入 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]` 即可免费使用搜索，无需额外 API Key。

目前 OpenClaw 已经对 xAI Grok 做了原生 web_search 透传（见源码 `runGrokSearch()` 中的 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`），但没有对 ZAI（GLM）和 Google（Gemini）做同样的支持。

当前要使用搜索功能只能配置 Brave/Perplexity/Grok 等付费第三方搜索 API，而 GLM 和 Gemini 自带的免费搜索被浪费了。

### Proposed solution

参考已有的 Grok 实现（`runGrokSearch()`），为 ZAI 和 Google provider 添加原生搜索工具透传：

1. **ZAI (GLM)**：当 provider 为 `zai` 时，在 API 请求 body 中自动加入 `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`
   - 文档：https://open.bigmodel.cn/dev/api/search/web-search-pro
   - 免费，无需额外 API Key

2. **Google (Gemini)**：当 provider 为 `google` 时，在请求中加入 `tools: [{ google_search: {} }]`
   - 文档：https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding
   - 免费，无需额外 API Key

可以通过配置项控制是否启用（如 `agents.defaults.nativeSearch: true`），在用户未配置第三方搜索 provider 时自动使用模型原生搜索作为 fallback。

相关 PR 参考：#10454、#13814

### Alternatives considered

_No response_

### Impact

受影响的用户/系统/渠道：
所有使用 ZAI（智谱 GLM）和 Google（Gemini）作为默认模型的 OpenClaw 用户。这两个 provider 是 OpenClaw 内置支持且推荐的免费/低成本模型。

严重程度：阻碍工作流程
Agent 在需要实时信息时（如查天气、新闻、技术文档、价格等）完全无法响应，只能回复"我无法搜索"。用户被迫手动搜索后粘贴给 agent，失去了 AI 助手的核心价值。

频率：总是
只要用户的模型是 GLM 或 Gemini，且没有额外配置付费第三方搜索 API（Brave/Perplexity），搜索功能就完全不可用。这不是偶发问题，而是 100% 复现。

后果：
- 用户体验严重下降：agent 无法回答任何需要实时数据的问题
- 额外的经济成本：用户被迫购买 Brave/Perplexity 等第三方搜索 API Key，而模型本身已经提供了免费搜索
- 不一致的体验：同样的 GLM-5 模型在智谱官网能搜索，在 OpenClaw 里却不能，造成用户困惑
- 已有先例未推广：OpenClaw 已为 Grok 实现了原生搜索透传，但未扩展到同样支持原生搜索的 GLM 和 Gemini

### Evidence/examples

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster
```
title: [Feature]: Support native web_search passthrough for ZAI (GLM) and Google (Gemini) providers

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a macOS default browser detection failure where LaunchServices stores a different bundle ID (e.g., `com.microsoft.edgemac`) than what `osascript`'s `path to application id` lookup expects (e.g., `com.microsoft.Edge`), causing the browser to not be detected even though it is installed and set as default. The fix introduces a `KNOWN_BUNDLE_ID_PATHS` map that covers LaunchServices-style bundle IDs for common Chromium-based browsers and updates `detectDefaultChromiumExecutableMac()` to fall back to these hardcoded paths whenever the `osascript`/`defaults` resolution chain fails. The fallback correctly validates file existence before accepting the path, and the two-stage approach (try dynamic resolution first, then fall back) is the right design. **Key changes:** - Adds `KNOWN_BUNDLE_ID_PATHS` mapping LaunchServices bundle IDs (e.g., `com.microsoft.edgemac*`, `com.brave.Browser`, `com.google.Chrome`, etc.) to their conventional `/Applications/...` executable paths. - Expands the early-exit `isKnownChromium` check to accept any bundle ID present in either `CHROMIUM_BUNDLE_IDS` or `KNOWN_BUNDLE_ID_PATHS`. - Restructures `detectDefaultChromiumExecutableMac` to attempt dynamic resolution first, then fall back to the map. **Minor gap:** The fallback map only covers system-wide `/Applications/...` installs. User-level installs under `~/Applications/...` (already handled by the static `findChromeExecutableMac`) are not covered, so the fallback could still miss browsers installed to the user's home directory.

The only notable limitation is that the hardcoded paths cover `/Applications/...` only and not `~/Applications/...` user installs, but this is a minor gap for the targeted edge case (Edge bundle ID mismatch) and the broader fallback (`findChromeExecutableMac`) still covers home-dir installs. - No files require special attention.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #10467: [Feature Request]: Multi-lane concurrency support for sub-agents via sessions_spawn
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 10467
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10467
- Title: [Feature Request]: Multi-lane concurrency support for sub-agents via sessions_spawn
- State: OPEN
- Author: lexobe
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Currently, all sub-agents spawned via the `sessions_spawn` tool are funneled into a single global queue lane: `subagent`. The concurrency is governed by a single limit `agents.defaults.subagents.maxConcurrent`.

This creates a bottleneck and reliability risk for complex multi-agent workflows (e.g., 'Evolution Engineering' patterns) where multiple independent paths of reasoning or tool usage are required.

## Problem

1. **Lane Exhaustion**: A burst of slow 'research' sub-agents can fill the `subagent` lane, blocking high-priority 'monitoring' or 'security' sub-agents.
2. **Lock Contention**: As noted in issue #4355, concurrent sub-agents often collide on session file locks. Named lanes would allow grouping related tasks to better manage these risks.
3. **Implicit Serialization**: Agents cannot currently specify parallel 'lanes' for different logic branches, forcing a flatter, more congested execution model.

## Proposed Solution

Add an optional `lane` parameter to the `sessions_spawn` tool.

```javascript
// Example usage in an agent turn
sessions_spawn(task: "Research market trends", lane: "research")
sessions_spawn(task: "Monitor security logs", lane: "security")
```

The OpenClaw gateway should:
1. Treat these as independent queue lanes.
2. Allow configuring `maxConcurrent` per lane in `openclaw.json`.
3. Fall back to the default `subagent` lane if no lane is specified.

## Benefits

- **Isolation**: Prevent low-priority background tasks from starving critical infrastructure sub-agents.
- **Scalability**: Support true 'multi-path' concurrent reasoning where an agent can spin up independent research and execution lanes.
- **Reliability**: Reduce cascading failures caused by a single slow task type saturating the global sub-agent pool.

## Environment
- OpenClaw version: 2026.2.3-1
- Use case: Multi-path autonomous evolution (Phase 110+ synthesis)
```
